entry.title: Hello World
entry.content: Welcome to Yum1era’s Blog!

entry.title: Mapping
entry.content:
Mapping:A mapping, or map, is a rule that assigns to each element of one set, called the domain, an element of another set, called the codomain.

Partial Mapping:A partial mapping is a mapping where some elements of the domain may not be assigned to any element in the codomain.

Total Mapping:A total mapping is a mapping where every element of the domain is assigned to exactly one element in the codemain.

Inverse Mapping:If there is a mapping $f$ from setA to setB,then its inverse mapping
$f^{-1}$ is a mapping from setB back to setA.The inverse mapping exists if and only if the original mapping $f$ is bijective,meaning it is both injective(one-to-one) and surjective(onto),For a bijective mapping $f$ ,its inverse $f^{-1}$ is also bijective,and for every element $y$ in B,there exists a unique element $x$ in A such that $f(x)=y$ and $f^{-1}(y)=x$
